component.name: SpectraControl
component.summary: Sync Philips Hue lights with your screen

description.p: SpectraControl is a Linux desktop app for Philips Hue. It samples colors from your monitor and pushes them to your Hue lights in real time over the Hue Entertainment API v2, with low enough latency for bias lighting and ambient effects.

ul.li: Real-time screen-to-light sync over Hue Entertainment v2 (DTLS).
Per-light monitor regions: bind any light to a specific rectangle of the screen.
Physical light positioning relative to the monitor for accurate sampling.
Audio-reactive mode that pulses brightness with system audio.
Dynamic, animated scenes with an in-app editor.
Real-time state sync from the bridge — external changes are reflected immediately.
System tray, desktop notifications and autostart.
Internationalization with a language picker on first run (English, Spanish).

description.p: Sync experience overhaul and Flatpak hardening:

ul.li: Sync intensity modes (Subtle/Medium/High/Intense) with non-linear gain curve.
Per-light spatial glow on the active zone card.
0-100 sliders and gamma curve for finer color response.
Optional shutdown toggle: turn lights off when closing while syncing.
Entertainment: break other active streams before claiming the bridge.
Flatpak manifest, AppStream metadata and offline build sources published as release assets.
In-app updater disabled inside the sandbox (Flathub owns updates).
